cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out if a man can legally leave all his money to his widow's sister, who he plans to marry next year. Let me break this down. First, I should consider the legal aspects of inheritance and marriage. In many places, the laws about who can inherit property from someone are pretty clear. Usually, if someone dies without a will, their closest relatives inherit, like a spouse, children, or parents. But if they have a will, they can usually leave their money to anyone they choose. Now, the man is currently married. That means his current wife would typically be the primary heir unless the will says otherwise. The man is saying he wants to leave everything to his future wife's sister, but he's not married to her yet. So, in a few months, he plans to marry someone else, his current wife's sister. That raises some interesting points. First, can a person make a will that leaves property to someone they haven't married yet? That might be a problem. Because
